m_Item1.QuestionText: If football is called cricket, cricket is called basketball , basketball is called badminton,  badminton is called volleyball, volleyball is called hockey and hockey is called golf then which of the following games is not played using a ball?
m_Item1.Explanation: badminton is played without any ball and in the question badminton is called vollyeball.

m_Item1.QuestionText: One rabbit saw 6 elephants while going towards River. Every elephant saw 2 monkeys are going towards river. Every monkey holds one tortoice in their hands.How many animals are going towards the river?
m_Item1.Explanation:
1 rabbit is going towards river not the six eephants. And these 6 elephants saw 2 monkeys are going towards river. Each monkey is holding 1 tortoice.

Hence, number of animals going towards river are 1 rabbit, 2 monkeys and 2 tortoice

= 1 + 2 + 2 = 5.

m_Item1.QuestionText: What is black when you buy it, red when you use it, and gray when you throw it away?
m_Item1.Explanation: It is Charcoal which is black when we buy it or when not being used. It gets red in appearance when put in use means to say when burning. And eventually when it's been consumed by fire or after getting burned out or used up, changes into ashes which look grey in color.

m_Item1.QuestionText:
Key, Door, Lock, Room, Switch on
Arrange the words given above in a meaningful sequence.
m_Item1.Explanation:
The given words to be placed in a logical sequence. Here if you want to switch on a light or fan in a room, the sequence you follow must be like

Key,

Lock ,

Door,

Room, Swith on.

Take the key and open the lock of a door enter in a room and then swith on.

m_Item1.QuestionText: M scores more run than N but less than P. Q scores more than N but less than M. Who is the lowest scorer?
m_Item1.Explanation:
M > N
P > M > N
P > M > Q > N
So, N is the lowest scorer.

m_Item1.QuestionText:
Select a figure from amongst the Answer Figures which will continue the same series as established by the five Problem Figures.

   A         B          C         D       E             1        2         3         4         5
m_Item1.Explanation: In each step, element at the upper-right position gets enlarged, inverts vertically and reaches the lower-left corner; the existing element at the lower-left position, is lost and a new small element appears at the upper-right position.

m_Item1.QuestionText:
In each of the following questions, two statements numbered I and II are given. There may be cause and effect relationship between the two statements. These two statements may be the effect of the same cause or independent causes. These statements may be independent causes without having any relationship. Read both the statements in each question and mark your answer as

(A) If statement I is the cause and statement II is its effect;
(B) If statement II is the cause and statement I is its effect;
(C) If both the statements I and II are independent causes;
(D) If both the statements I and II are effects of independent causes; and
(E) If both the statements I and II are effects of some common cause.
Statements:
1. The prices of petrol and diesel in the domestic market have remained unchanged for the past few months.
2. The crude oil prices in the international market have gone up substantially in the last few months.

Options.OptionText: Statement I is the cause and statement II is its effect
Statement II is the cause and statement I is its effect
Both the statements I and II are independent causes
Both the statements I and II are effects of independent causes

m_Item1.Explanation: The prices of petrol and diesel being stagnant in the domestic market and the increase in the same in the international market must be backed by independent causes.

m_Item1.QuestionText:
All the trees in the park are flowering trees.
Some of the trees in the park are dogwoods.
All dogwoods in the park are flowering trees.
If the first two statements are true, the third statement is
m_Item1.Explanation: Since all the trees in the park are flowering trees according to the first statement, and some of these flowering trees are dogwoods according to the second statement, it follows logically that all dogwoods in the park are flowering trees.Therefore, the third statement is true.

m_Item1.QuestionText:
A fruit basket contains more apples than lemons.
There are more lemons in the basket than there are oranges.
The basket contains more apples than oranges.
If the first two statements are true, the third statement is
m_Item1.Explanation:
By first two statements
apples > lemons > oranges
Therefore, the third statement is true.

m_Item1.QuestionText: There are some boxes containing 15, 17, 34, 26, 28 and 39 balls of either red or blue in color. Some boxes contain only red balls and others contain only blue. One salesman sold one box out of them and then he said, "I have the same number of red balls left out as that of blue". Number of balls in the box he sold were:
m_Item1.Explanation:
There are 6 boxes and 159 balls.
He sold 39 balls then he has 60 balls of each colour and also
 15+ 17+28 = 34 + 26 = 60

m_Item1.QuestionText: You have 120 balls and a two pan balance. If one of the balls is lighter and rest all balls are of equal weight, in how many minimum weighing, you can identify the lighter ball?
m_Item1.Explanation: Split into three parts.

Statement III: College A has a more admissions than college C.
If the first two statements are true, the third statement is -
m_Item1.Explanation: A > B, B < C. It doesn’t give us clarity about the relation between A and C.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Four of the following five are alike in a certain way and so form a group. Which is the one that does not belong to that group?

Apartments in the Lake View cost less than apartments in the Garden View.
Apartments in the River View cost more than apartments in the Garden View.
Of the three apartment buildings, the River View costs the most.
If the first two statements are true, the third statement is :

Blueberries cost more than strawberries.
Blueberries cost less than raspberries.
Raspberries cost more than both strawberries and blueberries.
If the first two statements are true, the third statement is
m_Item1.Explanation:
Given
Raspberries  > Blueberries  > strawberries.
So option A is correct.

m_Item1.QuestionContext: Read the following questions and choose the conclusions that follow logically.

SubQuestions.QuestionText:
All cows are ravens. Some ravens are black.
I. Some cows are black
II. All ravens are cows
III. All cows are black

Only girls are pretty. No woman is a girl.
I. Some girls are woman
II. No woman is pretty
III. Some women are pretty

Most cabbages are greens. All greens are good for health.
I.  Some cabbages are good for health
II. Few greens are not cabbages
III. Many cabbages are good for health

All kings are madmen. All madmen are kings.
I. Only madmen are kings.
II. Only kings are madmen.
III. Some kings are both madmen and kings.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Four of the following five are alike in a certain way and hence form a group. Which one of the following does not belong to that group ?
m_Item1.Explanation:
63 is divisible by 7 and 9.
64 is divisible by 8.
39 is divisible by 3 and 9.
48 is divisible by 6 and 8.
79 doesn't seem to fit any clear pattern based on divisibility.
So, the group consists of numbers that are divisible by either 3, 6, 7, 8, or 9. The number that doesn't belong to this group is 79 because it doesn't fit the divisibility pattern like the other numbers. Therefore, 79 is the answer.

m_Item1.QuestionContext: Each question consists of five statements followed by options consisting of three statements put together in a specific order. Choose the option which indicates a valid argument, that is, where the third statement is a conclusion drawn from the preceding two statements.

m_Item1.QuestionContext: Each question has a set of four statements. Each statement has three segments. Choose the alternative where the third segment in the statement can be logically deduced using both the preceding two.

B. Some men are cops. All cops are brave. Some brave people are cops.
C. All cops are brave. Some men are cops. Some men are brave.
D. All actors are pretty; MD is not an actor; MD is not pretty.

A. Citizens of Yes Islands speak only the truth. Citizens of Yes Islands are young people. Young people speak only the truth.
B. Citizens of Yes Islands speak only the truth. Some Yes Islands are in the Atlantic. Some citizens of Yes Islands are in the Atlantic.
C. Citizens of Yes Islands speak only the truth. Some young people are citizens of Yes Islands. Some young people speak only the truth.
D. Some people speak only the truth. Some citizens of Yes Islands speak only the truth. Some people who speak only the truth are citizens of Yes Islands.

A. All mammals are viviparous. Some fish are viviparous. Some fish are mammals.
B. All birds are oviparous. Some fish are not oviparous. Some fish are birds.
C. No mammal is oviparous. Some creatures are oviparous and some are not. Some creatures are not mammals.
D. Some creatures are mammals. Some creatures are viviparous. Some mammals are viviparous.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Four of the following five are alike in a certain way and so form a group. Which is the one that does not belong to that group?
m_Item1.Explanation: In this context, "cooking" can be considered the odd one out because the other methods listed (baking, steaming, frying, boiling) are specific techniques of cooking, whereas "cooking" is a general term that encompasses various methods of preparing food.

m_Item1.Explanation: Because the first two statements are true, raspberries are the most expensive of the three.

m_Item1.QuestionText: A chain is broken into three pieces of equal lengths containing 3 links each. It is taken to a blacksmith to join into a single continuous one. How many links are to  be opened to make it?
m_Item1.Explanation:
Total links = Link in one chain * total chains= 3*3 = 9

But two join these three chains together goldsmith needs to open only 2 links.

By opening one link chain number 1 and 2 will be joined and then by opening another link, third chain will also be joined.

Hence, the answer is 2.

(B) If only (2) conclusion follows
(C) If either (1) or (2) follows
(D) If neither (1) nor (2) follows and
(E) If both (1) and (2) follow.

m_Item1.QuestionText:
Among A, B, C, D, E and F, who is the heaviest ?
Statements:
I.    A and D are heavier than B, E and F but none of them is the heaviest.
II.    A is heavier than D but lighter than C.

Options.OptionText: I alone is sufficient while II alone is not sufficient
II alone is sufficient while I alone is not sufficient
Either I or II is sufficient
Neither I nor II is sufficient
Both I and II are sufficient

m_Item1.Explanation: From I, we conclude that since none of A and D is the heaviest and each one of B, E and F is lighter than both A and D, so C is the heaviest.
